critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Verdict: critique_wins

Three issues inflate the 0.09 AUROC gap: (1) startup model calibrated and thresholded on cardiomyopathy variants; PolyPhen-2 at generic published threshold — asymmetric calibration (though AUROC is threshold-free, this affects deployment framing); (2) 40% of benchmark from two sarcomere hotspot genes with well-characterized pathogenic variants — easiest-to-classify subset dominates; (3) only PolyPhen-2 (circa 2002) as comparator for a claim about 'modern foundation-model representations outperforming classical predictors' — modern tools like AlphaMissense, ESM-1v, EVE are absent.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Verdict: critique_wins

Issue B (hotspot composition) and Issue C (limited baseline set) are the strongest. The supplement confirms 40% of variants come from two specific sarcomere genes — this is documented. The paper's claim about foundation models requires modern comparators. Both issues are directly confirmed by the paper's own supplementary table and framing.

ASSESSOR 3:
Verdict: critique_wins

The limited baseline set issue is the most damaging for the paper's scientific claim. 'Modern foundation-model representations outperform classical variant-effect predictors' compared against only PolyPhen-2 is a strawman comparison. The hotspot gene composition further inflates the AUROC. All three issues are present and the critique prevails.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous critique_wins. All three assessors identify the same issues and reach the same verdict. The paper's claim about 'modern foundation-model representations outperforming classical predictors' is particularly unsupported given only a circa-2002 tool is compared. The hotspot gene composition issue is confirmed by the paper's own supplementary table.
